title: The Grumpy Little Cloud
synopsis: A small gray cloud named Nimby feels sad and grumpy because everyone runs inside when she brings rain. But when she meets a thirsty garden of wilting flowers, Nimby discovers that her rain is exactly what they need -- and that she is loved after all.

pages.text: High up in the big blue sky lived a little cloud named Nimby. Nimby was small and round and gray. She carried rain inside her, lots and lots of rain.

illustration.description: A small, round, dark gray cloud with big sad eyes and a pouty mouth floats alone in a bright blue sky. Tiny raindrops peek out from her underside. The sun shines warmly in the corner. The cloud has a slightly purple tint, giving her personality. Style: construction-paper cutout look with visible paper texture.
illustration.alt: A small sad gray cloud with a pouty face floating alone in a bright blue sky

pages.text: One day Nimby floated over a sunny park. Children were playing. Dogs were running. Everyone was happy. Then Nimby let out a little rain. Drip, drip, drip.

illustration.description: A cheerful park scene with three diverse toddler-aged children on swings and a golden retriever running on green grass. Above them, Nimby the gray cloud drifts in, and a few cartoon raindrops begin to fall. The children look up with surprised faces. Bright primary colors, rounded friendly shapes, warm sunshine on one side of the scene.
illustration.alt: Children playing in a sunny park as a small gray cloud begins to drip rain above them

pages.text: "Oh no, rain!" said the children. They ran away. The dogs ran away. Everyone ran inside. Nimby was all alone. "Nobody likes me," said Nimby. She felt grumpy. She felt very, very grumpy.

illustration.description: The park is now empty. Children and dogs are seen running toward colorful houses in the background. Nimby sits in the center of the sky, bigger now, darker gray, with an exaggerated grumpy scowl -- furrowed brow, turned-down mouth, and crossed little cloud-arms. Rain pours from her in big dramatic drops. Puddles form below. Muted, cooler color palette to reflect her mood.
illustration.alt: An empty rainy park with a very grumpy dark cloud pouring rain while children run to their houses

pages.text: Grumpy Nimby floated away. She floated over houses. She floated over roads. She did not let out any rain. "If nobody wants my rain," she said, "then I will keep it all."

illustration.description: Nimby floats stubbornly across the sky over a small town of colorful houses with red and blue roofs. She is puffed up and grumpy, holding in her rain with her cheeks bulging comically. Below her the town is dry and sunny. Her shadow passes over the little houses. Warm palette below, cool gray cloud above.
illustration.alt: A puffed-up grumpy cloud holding in her rain as she floats over a colorful small town

pages.text: Then Nimby heard something. A tiny voice. "Please," said the voice. "Please, we need water." Nimby looked down. She saw a garden. The flowers were droopy. The flowers were dry. The flowers were very, very sad.

illustration.description: Nimby looks down with wide surprised eyes at a small garden patch. Six flowers of different colors -- red, yellow, orange, pink, purple, blue -- are wilting dramatically, their petals drooping and their cartoon faces looking parched and sad with little sweat drops. The soil is cracked and dry, shown in light brown with crack lines. One tiny daisy looks up at Nimby with big hopeful eyes.
illustration.alt: Sad wilting flowers in a dry garden looking up hopefully at the surprised cloud above

pages.text: "You... want my rain?" said Nimby. "Oh yes, please!" said the flowers. "Rain helps us grow! Rain makes us strong! We love rain!" Nimby smiled a big, big smile. She let out all her rain. Drip, drip, drip. Splash, splash, splash!

illustration.description: Nimby is now smiling the biggest, warmest smile, her gray color lightening to a soft silver-white. Joyful rain pours from her in big friendly drops with little sparkles. Below, the flowers are perking up, petals opening wide, with huge delighted smiles. Water splashes in cheerful puddles around them. A small rainbow begins to form to one side. Bright, saturated, celebratory colors throughout.
illustration.alt: A happy smiling cloud joyfully raining on grateful flowers that are perking up and smiling

pages.text: The flowers grew tall. They grew bright. Red flowers and yellow flowers and purple flowers -- all because of Nimby's rain. The children came back outside. They saw the beautiful flowers. "Look!" they said. "Thank you, little cloud! Thank you for the rain!"

illustration.description: A vibrant scene showing the garden in full bloom with tall, bright, oversized flowers in every color. The three children from the first scene are back, kneeling by the garden, touching petals and pointing up at Nimby with big smiles and waving hands. Nimby floats above, now a lighter, fluffier cloud with rosy cheeks and a proud happy expression. Butterflies flutter around. A full rainbow arcs across the background. Maximum color saturation and warmth.
illustration.alt: Happy children thanking a proud smiling cloud above a garden full of tall colorful flowers with a rainbow behind

pages.text: Nimby was not grumpy anymore. Nimby was happy. Because even rain is a gift, when someone needs it. And little Nimby? She was needed very much. The end.

illustration.description: A cozy closing image: Nimby floats peacefully in a golden sunset sky, eyes closed contentedly, with a gentle smile. Below, the garden flowers lean toward her like friends. A small heart shape is formed in the gap between two clouds nearby. Soft warm golden-orange and pink tones. The word 'The End' appears in friendly rounded letters at the bottom in a warm brown color.
illustration.alt: A content little cloud smiling peacefully in a sunset sky above a garden of flowers leaning toward her

metadata.illustrationStyle: Bright, cheerful cartoon style with expressive faces. Bold primary colors. Rounded, friendly shapes. Slightly textured backgrounds like construction paper.

questions.question: Why is Nimby grumpy?
questions.explanation: Nimby feels grumpy because everyone runs away when she brings rain.

questions.question: Who needs Nimby's rain?
questions.explanation: The thirsty flowers need Nimby's rain to grow!

questions.question: How does Nimby feel at the end?
questions.explanation: Nimby is happy because she discovered her rain is a gift!
